description: Replace all stub implementations with real SDK integrations: Stripe in payments, FFmpeg in media, FCM/APNs in notifications, CloudFront CDN in media, MeiliSearch in search, Prisma+Redis in social-graph, Redis auction in quantads, pgvector matching in quantmax, and SMTP inbound service.
steps: STEP 1 - STRIPE: Replace packages/payments/src/services/gateway-service.ts with a real Stripe SDK integration. Create a StripeGateway class that wraps the Stripe SDK with methods: createPaymentIntent(amount, currency, customerId, metadata), createCustomer(email, name, metadata), refund(paymentIntentId, amount?), createSubscription(customerId, priceId), verifyWebhook(payload, signature). Use env vars STRIPE_SECRET_KEY and STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET. Add a co-located gateway-service.test.ts that mocks the stripe module and tests each method.

STEP 2 - FFMPEG: Replace packages/media/src/services/video-transcoder.ts with a real fluent-ffmpeg based implementation. VideoTranscoder class with transcode(inputPath, outputDir, profiles) that produces HLS .m3u8 output using fluent-ffmpeg, generateHLS(inputPath, outputDir, segmentDuration) for HLS segments, and extractThumbnail(inputPath, outputPath, timestamp). Wire through @quant/queue by adding a TranscodeJobSchema to packages/queue/src/job-definitions.ts. Add video-transcoder.test.ts that mocks fluent-ffmpeg.

STEP 3 - FCM/APNs: Replace packages/notifications/src/services/push-service.ts with real Firebase Admin SDK (FCM) and @parse/node-apn (APNs). PushService class with initialize(firebaseCredentials, apnOptions), sendPush(token, platform, payload) that routes to FCM for android/web and APNs for ios, sendMulticast(tokens, payload). Use env vars FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT, APN_KEY_ID, APN_TEAM_ID. Graceful error handling (no 500s on send failure). Add push-service.test.ts mocking firebase-admin and @parse/node-apn.

STEP 4 - SOCIAL GRAPH: Create packages/social-graph/src/services/social-graph.service.ts using Prisma (UserRelationship model already exists) and Redis (ioredis). SocialGraphService class with constructor(prisma, redis). Methods: follow(followerId, followingId), unfollow(followerId, followingId), getFollowers(userId, pagination), getFollowing(userId, pagination), getMutualFollowers(userId1, userId2), suggestFriendsOfFriends(userId, limit), block(blockerId, blockedId), mute(muterId, mutedId). Redis caches follower counts, mutual followers. Update packages/social-graph/src/index.ts to export the new service. Do NOT delete graph-store.ts (keep for backward compat) but ensure new service uses no in-memory Map for core data. Add social-graph.service.test.ts with mock Prisma and mock Redis.

STEP 5 - MEILISEARCH: Create packages/search/src/services/search-client.ts. SearchClient class with constructor(host, apiKey). Methods: ensureIndex(indexName, primaryKey, searchableAttributes, filterableAttributes), indexDocument(indexName, document), indexBatch(indexName, documents), search(indexName, query, options?), deleteDocument(indexName, documentId). Create indexes for: emails, messages, posts, videos, users, files. Update packages/search/src/index.ts to export SearchClient. Add search-client.test.ts mocking meilisearch.

STEP 6 - SMTP INBOUND: Create services/smtp-inbound/src/main.ts. Use SMTPServer from smtp-server to accept inbound email. Parse with mailparser. On message received, validate, extract fields (from, to, subject, html, text, attachments), and (stub) write to database. Create services/smtp-inbound/src/smtp-server.ts with SmtpInboundServer class (start, stop, onMessage callback). Create services/smtp-inbound/src/sender.ts with EmailSender class using nodemailer (transport config from env: SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT or default to Mailhog in dev). Add services/smtp-inbound/src/main.test.ts and smtp-server.test.ts.

STEP 7 - REDIS AUCTION: Create apps/quantads/backend/services/auction.service.ts. AuctionService class with constructor(redis: Redis). Methods: submitBid(auctionId, bidderId, bidAmount, qualityScore) using ZADD with effective score (bid * qualityScore), resolveAuction(auctionId) implementing second-price auction via ZREVRANGE (winner pays second-highest bid), getActiveAuctions(), cancelBid(auctionId, bidderId). Add auction.service.test.ts with mock Redis (vi.fn() on zadd, zrevrange, zrem, etc.).

STEP 8 - PGVECTOR MATCHING: Update apps/quantmax/backend/services/matching.service.ts. Add generateEmbedding(profile: DatingProfile) method that produces a numeric vector from profile interests/preferences (can use a simple deterministic embedding for now). Add findMatches(userId, limit) that queries using pgvector cosine similarity (<=> operator) via Prisma.$queryRaw. The DatingProfile model already exists in schema; add an 'embedding' Unsupported("vector(256)") field to the Prisma schema if feasible (or use $queryRaw with raw SQL). Add/update matching.service.test.ts.

STEP 9 - CLOUDFRONT CDN: Replace packages/media/src/services/cdn-service.ts with real AWS CloudFront SDK. CDNService class using @aws-sdk/client-cloudfront and @aws-sdk/cloudfront-signer. Methods: getPresignedUrl(key, expiresInSeconds) using getSignedUrl from cloudfront-signer, invalidate(paths: string[]) using CreateInvalidationCommand. Config from env: CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID, CLOUDFRONT_KEY_PAIR_ID, CLOUDFRONT_PRIVATE_KEY, CLOUDFRONT_DOMAIN. Add cdn-service.test.ts mocking the AWS SDK.
acceptance_criteria: packages/social-graph/src/services/social-graph.service.ts uses Prisma + Redis (no in-memory Map for core data storage)
Every new/replaced service file has a co-located .test.ts file
verification: grep -r 'new Map' packages/social-graph/src/services/ should return 0 results
grep -r 'import Stripe' packages/payments/src/services/gateway-service.ts confirms Stripe SDK usage
